'''Clan Beviin''' was clan of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] in existence by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Following the [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] of [[Goran Beviin]] and [[Medrit Vasur]], the two [[Gender/Legends|male]] Mandalorians lived together at the [[Beviin-Vasur farm]] on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], ten [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] outside of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]]. Vasur worked as a [[Metalsmith/Legends|blacksmith]] and Beviin as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], supplementing the living they made from working the [[Farm/Legends|farm]] together. During the war with the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]], Beviin fought with the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] under [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] against the invaders, eventually [[Gai bal manda|adopting]] fellow Mandalorian [[Dinua Jeban]] after her [[Parent/Legends|mother]] [[Briika Jeban|Briika]] [[Death/Legends|died]] in [[Battle of New Holgha|battle]] with the Yuuzhan Vong.

After the war came to an [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|end]] and the Yuuzhan Vong were defeated, Jeban continued to live with her adopted parents at their farm on Mandalore. Over time, she met and married the Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Jintar]], and the couple had two [[Youngling/Legends|children]], a boy named [[Shalk]] and a girl named [[Briila]]. By the start of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], a conflict that broke out between the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and the [[Confederation]], the Beviin clan had taken to allowing ''Mand'alor'' Fett to reside alongside their family at the Beviin-Vasur farm, while he attempted to get acquainted with the culture and people he had been estranged from for most of his life.

==History==
Clan Beviin was a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] clan living on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]].<ref name="APM" /> Following the [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] of [[Goran Beviin]] and [[Medrit Vasur]],<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> the two Mandalorian men settled down together on a [[Beviin-Vasur farm|farm]] both [[Gender/Legends|men]] jointly owned, located ten [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] outside of Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /> The couple worked the [[Farm/Legends|farm]] together,<ref name="APM" /> though Vasur was an educated [[Metallurgy/Legends|metallurgist]] who also worked as a [[Metalsmith/Legends|blacksmith]],<ref name="Sacrifice" /> and Beviin was a veteran [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] who took [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] work to supplement their income.<ref name="APM" />

In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Beviin and Vasur's farm was burdened with a poor season, forcing Beviin to accept jobs he normally wouldn't, including an [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] contract on a [[Tholote B'Leph|politician]] from [[Ter Abbes]], offered by an individual known as Udelen. In truth, Udelen turned out to be the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Nom Anor]], who through Beviin, came to contract the services of numerous Mandalorian mercenaries in the lead-up to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong's invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During the war against the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] invaders, Beviin fought alongside the reigning [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]; publicly, the Mandalorian [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|soldiers]] had allied with the Yuuzhan Vong, while secretly, they sought to [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] their efforts and covertly gather intelligence. During the [[Battle of New Holgha|attack]] on [[New Holgha]], Beviin engaged a Yuuzhan Vong [[subaltern]] named [[Bur'lorr]] in order to rescue a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] whom the Mandalorians could pass their acquired information to. Though he was able to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the Yuuzhan Vong [[Warrior caste|warrior]] with a pair of [[Crushgaunt/Legends|crushgaunts]] he owned, Beviin's Mandalorian comrade [[Briika Jeban]] was mortally wounded in the skirmish. Having made her a promise [[Standard month/Legends|months]] earlier, upon her death Beviin [[Gai bal manda|adopted]] Jeban's orphaned daughter, [[Dinua Jeban|Dinua]], welcoming the young woman into his clan.<ref name="APM" />

Both Beviin and his adopted daughter continued to fight the Yuuzhan Vong during the war,<ref name="Sacrifice" /> and Beviin participated in the [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|defense]] of Mandalore when the [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] attacked the Mandalorian [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref> Following the war's end, Jeban came to live with Beviin and Vasur at their farm. She later met and married the Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Jintar]], and Clan Beviin soon grew as the couple had two [[Youngling/Legends|children]] of their own, a daughter named [[Briila]] and a son named [[Shalk]].<ref name="Sacrifice" />

In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the Beviin clan had taken to allowing ''Mand'alor'' Boba Fett to stay with them on their farm. Fett, who counted Goran Beviin among his few friends in life, lived out of his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], though he occasionally joined the family for meals at their house. As the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] broke out between the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and the [[Confederation]], Jintar sought mercenary work in the war; he suffered an injury while away, and returned to the clan not long after.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> By [[41 ABY]], Clan Beviin continued to extend its hospitality not only to Fett, but Fett's estranged wife, [[Sintas Vel]], who was recovering from blindness and [[Amnesia/Legends|amnesia]] following [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of being frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. Around the same [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] had come to Mandalore, seeking Fett's aid in capturing or killing her [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] brother, [[Darth Caedus]]. As a favor to Fett, Beviin allowed Solo to stay with his clan at their farm, along with Vel. Beviin later took part in training Solo in Mandalorian fighting techniques,<ref name="Revelation" /> while his husband Medrit<ref name="TCSWE" /> contributed to looking after the recovering Vel.<ref name="Revelation" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Goran Beviin and Medrit Vasur are a gay couple. Homosexual men. Call it what you like, they're in a same-sex marriage.|[[Karen Traviss]], discussing the gay married couple at the head of Clan Beviin|<ref name="Traviss web">{{WebCite|url=https://www.karentraviss.com/page10/files/Is_it_true_that_some_of_the_Man.html|text=Is it true that some of the Mandalorians in your LEGACY OF THE FORCE books are gay?|author=[[Karen Traviss]]|archivedate=20120830234642|nolive=1}}</ref>}}
The first hints of Clan Beviin came in the [[2006]] [[EBook|e-novella]] ''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'', by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]]. In it, Traviss introduced the characters of Goran Beviin, his spouse Medrit, and their adopted daughter, Dinua Jeban, into ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe.<ref name="APM">''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''</ref> Beviin appeared again in Traviss' first entry into the multi-author novel series, ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'', entitled [[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines|''Bloodlines'']],<ref name="Bloodlines">''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''</ref> but it was her second ''Legacy of the Force'' novel, [[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice|''Sacrifice'']], that featured Clan Beviin in full. Medrit was revealed to be Beviin's husband, establishing one of the first gay couples in the ''Star Wars'' franchise. ''Sacrifice'' also reintroduced an adult Dinua Jeban, her own husband Jintar, and their two young children, Briika and Shalk, to Clan Beviin.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> The entire clan again featured in Traviss' third ''Legacy of the Force'' novel, entitled [[Legacy of the Force: Revelation|''Revelation'']].<ref name="Revelation" />
